title: Iqbal Verse #250 — Allama Iqbal Urdu Shayari
content:
وہ مُلتفت ہوں تو کُنجِ قفَس بھی آزادی
نہ ہوں تو صحنِ چمن بھی مقامِ مجبوری

— Translation —
Bondage is freedom With favours from on high,
And when favours are withheld, Even freedom is bondage.
meaning: "Iqbal Verse #250" by Allama Iqbal is a inspirational urdu poem consisting of 6 lines. This Urdu verse by Allama Iqbal showcases the rich tradition of South Asian poetry. The poet masterfully employs Urdu literary conventions to convey profound emotional depth. Beginning with "وہ مُلتفت ہوں تو کُنجِ قفَس بھی آزادی نہ ہوں تو صحنِ چمن بھی مقامِ مجبوری...", this piece explores themes of inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional resonance. The work invites contemplation on the deeper currents of life, love, and the human condition. Allama Iqbal's celebrated body of poetry continues to inspire readers across generations and cultures, and this particular work stands as a powerful example of their artistic vision.
